overview: Aligns text units from two input files by matching their ids (taken from `TextUnit.getName()`). Works with any filter that produces unique names for its text units, such as the Properties Filter. Can either produce bilingual text units in the pipeline or generate a TMX file output. Both inputs must be non-segmented.

generateTMX.help:
Controls the step's output mode:

- **Checked**: Produces a TMX file at the configured path. The filter events passed downstream are **unchanged** (the alignment results go only to the TMX file).
- **Unchecked**: Each text unit in the returned events is replaced with a new bilingual text unit containing aligned source and target content.

Text units present in the source but missing from the target generate a warning. Text units in the target but not in the source are ignored.
notes: For target `<tuv>` data to be generated in the TMX, the **Copy to/over the target** option must also be checked.

dependsOn.condition: must also be enabled for TMX target <tuv> data to be generated

tmxOutputPath.help: Full path for the generated TMX file. Only used when **Generate a TMX file** is enabled.
tmxOutputPath.values: A file system path (absolute or relative) ending in `.tmx`.

replaceWithSource.help: When the target file has no corresponding entry for a source text unit, the source text is used as the target instead of leaving it empty. Useful as a fallback to ensure every text unit has target content.

copyToTarget.help:
Copies the aligned target content directly onto the text unit's target. If the entry from the target file is marked as *approved*, that property is preserved and passed along.

**Use with caution** — this overwrites any existing target content and the result will not be segmented.
notes: **WARNING**: Any existing target content will be lost, and the copied target will not be segmented.
Required in combination with **Generate a TMX file** for target `<tuv>` elements to appear in the TMX output.

storeAsAltTranslation.help: Attaches the matched target as an `AltTranslation` annotation on the text unit. This makes the alignment result visible to subsequent pipeline steps (e.g., leveraging steps) without overwriting the existing target.

suppressTusWithNoTarget.help: Filters out text units for which no matching target was found, preventing them from being passed to downstream steps. Useful when you only want to process fully aligned entries.

useTextUnitIds.help: Switches alignment matching from `TextUnit.getName()` (resource name) to `TextUnit.getId()`. Use this when your filter produces meaningful text unit IDs rather than resource names for matching.

limitations: Assumes each text unit has a **unique** name value (`TextUnit.getName()`). The filter used must produce unique names for all text units in the documents.
Aligns at the **text unit** level only — does not align segments within text units.
For multilingual target files (e.g., XLIFF), alignment requires both matching id **and** matching source text between the two text units; if source texts differ, no alignment is made.
processingNotes: The **first** input file provides the source content; the **second** input file provides the target content.
If the target file is **monolingual** (e.g., Java properties), the source extracted from that file is used as target content.
If the target file is **multilingual** (e.g., XLIFF), the actual target extracted from that file is used as target content, and an additional source-text equality check is performed.
Text units present in source but missing from target generate a **warning**. Text units in target but not in source are silently **ignored**.

examples.title: Aligning two properties files into bilingual text units
examples.description: Aligns a source and target Java properties file by matching property names, producing bilingual text units for downstream processing.
examples.config:
```yaml
generateTMX: false
storeAsAltTranslation: true
replaceWithSource: false
suppressTusWithNoTarget: false
```

examples.title: Generating a TMX file from aligned properties
examples.description: Produces a TMX file from two aligned properties files. Both `generateTMX` and `copyToTarget` must be enabled for target `<tuv>` elements to appear in the TMX.
